mnemonics: EXterior = EXcluded (point is outside the set and its boundary)
Boundary = Both Sides (every neighborhood has points inside AND outside the set)
keyPoints: An exterior point of a set S has a neighborhood that contains NO points of S.
A boundary point of a set S has neighborhoods that ALWAYS contain points both in S and not in S.
The exterior of a set is the interior of its complement.
commonMistakes: Confusing boundary points with limit points; a boundary point doesn't have to be in the set.
Assuming a point can't be both a boundary and an interior point; they are mutually exclusive.
Thinking the exterior is just the complement; it's specifically the interior of the complement.
